description_long: This object is a standalone basketball hoop structure commonly found in urban parks or recreational areas. It features a fan-shaped, slightly weathered backboard made of a light-colored material, mounted on a sturdy, curved metal pole. The orange basketball rim is attached to the front of the backboard, with no visible net. The support pole is robust and appears to be anchored into the ground, designed to withstand outdoor conditions. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing durability and function over decorative elements. The structure is suitable for single-hoop basketball play, often seen in public spaces or street courts.
description: A weathered, fan-shaped basketball backboard with an orange rim mounted on a sturdy, curved metal pole, typical of urban outdoor courts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the fan-shaped backboard and orange rim clearly, with the pole directly behind.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved metal pole and the protruding orange rim.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the backboard and the full length of the support pole.
description_view_3: Angled side view showing the depth of the backboard and the curvature of the pole.
